key_insight: SAGEAgent learns to decide, step by step, whether the next diagnostic modality is worth the added burden for survival prediction.
clinical_relevance: It could help oncology workflows tailor workups by reducing unnecessary testing while preserving prediction performance.
method: LLM-based agent for sequential modality acquisition
Uses clinical tools to convert numeric outputs into text
Combines episodic memory of similar cases with semantic memory of reusable decision patterns
evidence_signal: In a glioma cohort spanning TCGA-LGG, TCGA-GBM, and BraTS with four modalities, the approach matched competitive survival prediction accuracy while cutting average acquisition burden by 55%.
watch_item: Performance depends on the ordered availability of modalities and retrospective cohort data, so real-world validation is needed before workflow use.
paper_title: SAGEAgent: A Self-Evolving Agent for Cost-Aware Modality Acquisition in Multimodal Survival Prediction
